meta.name: CORE-10 Clinical Outcomes in Routine Evaluation
meta.description: The CORE-10 is a brief 10-item self-report scale that assesses the overall level of psychological distress. It covers anxiety, depression, trauma, physical functioning, social functioning, and risk, widely used in routine outcome monitoring in clinical practice.
meta.author: Connell & Barkham (2007)
meta.reference: Connell J, Barkham M. CORE-10 User Manual, Version 1.1. CORE System Trust, 2007.
meta.instruction: Please select the option that best describes how you have been over the last week.

commonChoices.label: Most or all of the time

questions.text: I have felt tense, anxious, or nervous
I have felt I have someone to turn to for support when needed
I have felt unable to cope when things go wrong
Talking to people, walking, or getting dressed has been slower than usual
I have lost interest in life
I have been angry at myself
I have felt OK about myself physically and have had enough energy
I have not wanted to be with people
I have felt low or unhappy
Unwanted thoughts of ending my life have come into my mind

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your psychological distress level is within the normal range. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good mental health
Keep up healthy habits and social activities
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Mild Distress
ranges.description: You are experiencing some psychological distress that may have a mild impact on daily life. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Pay attention to your emotional changes
Learn stress management and relaxation techniques
Maintain social connections
Consider seeking professional help if distress persists
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise

ranges.label: Moderate Distress
ranges.description: Your psychological distress level is elevated and may noticeably affect work and daily functioning. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider seeking counseling or therapy
Share your feelings with someone you trust
Maintain regular routines and physical exercise
Avoid unhealthy coping mechanisms
Consider consulting a mental health professional

ranges.label: Severe Distress
ranges.description: Your psychological distress level is very high. Daily functioning may be significantly affected. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional mental health help immediately
If you have thoughts of self-harm, please contact a crisis helpline
Ensure you have trusted people around for support
Seeking help is a sign of strength, not weakness
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
